info.name: ServiceScheduler Dev
info.description:
Manual smoke-test suite for the Appointment Booking slice.

- **Auth** — /api/auth/login, /register, /logout, /profile/me (cookie-based JWT).
    
- **Booking — Happy path** — POST /api/appointments with seeded data.
    
- **Booking — Issue #4 validation** — 5 error branches + 2 guard-order checks.
    
- **Booking — Conflict handling (#5/#6/#7)** — availability narrowing, half-open intervals, DB EXCLUDE constraint, graceful retry on race.
    
- **Auth boundary** — 401 without cookie.
    

Requires the 'ServiceScheduler Dev' environment (all seed GUIDs and dev credentials are stored there).

Conflict-handling tests use pre-request scripts to compute unique future times per run, so you do NOT need to TRUNCATE appointments between runs.

request.description: Creates a fresh account with the "user" role. Anonymous endpoint. Pre-request script generates a unique email per run so re-running does not hit "email already taken".

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (did you login first?)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Rotates the refresh-token cookie. Browser (and Postman with cookie jar enabled) only sends the refresh cookie to this exact path — it is scoped Path=/api/auth/refresh. Requires the refresh cookie from a prior successful Login. On success both cookies are re-issued; on any failure both are cleared and 401 is returned.

exec: // Idempotent across runs: pick a random future day/hour so the DB doesn't accumulate collisions.

item.name: Book brake inspection at D2 - Civic (201)

exec: // Idempotent across runs: random future day/hour, so DB collisions never accumulate.
pm.test('Emily Davis assigned (only D2 tech qualified for brakes)', () => pm.expect(body.technician.name).to.equal('Emily Davis'));

request.description: Exercises the newly-seeded second dealership (Shelbyville North), the Civic, and the Brake inspection service type. Emily Davis is the only D2 technician qualified for brakes, so the handler must pick her.

item.name: Book wheel alignment on F-150 at D1 (201)

request.description: Exercises the F-150 and the Wheel alignment service type (90 min). At D1 both Sam and Maria are qualified for alignment, so Sam (first candidate at D1) is expected.

item.name: Book full service on Civic at D1 (201, captures lastAppointmentId)

exec: // Fixed 09:00 UTC on a random future day. Also persisted as a collection variable so the
// Reschedule below can compute a new time relative to it.
pm.test('Maria Garcia assigned (only D1 tech qualified for full service)', () => pm.expect(body.technician.name).to.equal('Maria Garcia'));
// Capture the id so Reschedule / Cancel below can act on it.

request.description: Exercises the Full service type (120 min). At D1 only Maria is qualified for full service. The test script captures body.appointmentId into the collection variable {{lastAppointmentId}} so the Reschedule and Cancel requests below can operate on it — run this before them (Runner order in this folder does this automatically).

exec: // Pick a new time 7 days after the original booking (still 09:00 UTC).
throw new Error('happy_fullservice_start not set. Run "Book full service on Civic at D1" first.');
pm.test('scheduledStart moved to the new time', () => {

request.description: Uses {{lastAppointmentId}} captured by 'Book full service on Civic at D1' above. Shifts the appointment 7 days later at the same 09:00 UTC — availability query re-runs, Maria should still be free, so 200 with the new scheduledStart.

Reschedule refuses a past newStart. Unlike RequestAppointment (400 REQUESTED_START_IN_PAST), the reschedule handler returns 409 APPOINTMENT_IN_PAST — this is a lifecycle guard for an already-created appointment, not request validation.

Cancels the appointment captured by 'Book full service on Civic at D1'. Cancel is a soft state transition (Status = Cancelled), NOT a row delete — the row stays for history and simply drops out of the availability query and the partial EXCLUDE constraint. First send → 204 No Content. Every send after that → 409 APPOINTMENT_ALREADY_CANCELLED (cancel is one-way, not idempotent), so re-sending in isolation without re-running Book is expected to return 409.

Second cancel on the same {{lastAppointmentId}} — the aggregate's Cancel() throws AppointmentAlreadyCancelledException which the handler maps to 409 APPOINTMENT_ALREADY_CANCELLED. Idempotent no-op is NOT the design here; cancellation is a one-way state transition and a repeat is a caller error.
Cancel handler's not-found guard: no row with the id — 404 APPOINTMENT_NOT_FOUND. This must fire BEFORE the ownership check so the API doesn't leak whether an id exists to non-owners.

exec: pm.test('First send: Alex Chen assigned', () => pm.expect(body.technician.name).to.equal('Alex Chen'));
pm.test('Repeat send: code is a conflict (NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN or NO_BAY_AVAILABLE)', () => {

request.description: Fixed slot: 2027-01-15 08:00 UTC. First Send = 201 (Alex Chen assigned). Every Send after that = 409 NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN because Alex is now booked at that time and Sam is the only other qualified tech but will be busy after the second attempt too. The test accepts both status codes and asserts the appropriate response body for each.

pm.test('Repeat send: code = NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN (Maria is the only D1 full-service tech)', () => {

request.description: Fixed slot: 2027-01-15 10:00 UTC. Maria Garcia is the ONLY D1 tech qualified for full service, so second Send = 409. On success, captures the id into {{lastAppointmentIdFixed}} for the Reschedule and Cancel requests below to use — note: distinct from the {{lastAppointmentId}} used by the random-slots folder, so both folders can coexist.

item.name: Reschedule appointment to fixed target (200 first / 409 after cancel)

exec: pm.test('scheduledEnd = start + 120min (BR-07: duration re-derived from service)', () => {
pm.test('Repeat send: code = APPOINTMENT_ALREADY_CANCELLED (appointment was cancelled)', () => {

request.description: Requires 'Book full service on Civic at D1' to have succeeded (captured {{lastAppointmentIdFixed}}). Moves the appointment to 2027-01-22 10:00 UTC. Send once → 200 (moved). Send twice → still 200 (moving to same time is idempotent). But if 'Cancel' runs between sends, this returns 409 APPOINTMENT_ALREADY_CANCELLED — reschedule requires Confirmed status.

Requires 'Book full service on Civic at D1' to have captured {{lastAppointmentIdFixed}}. First Send → 204 (soft cancel: Status → Cancelled). Every Send after that → 409 APPOINTMENT_ALREADY_CANCELLED because cancel is a one-way state transition, not idempotent.

exec: pm.test('Status is 403 (did you log in as admin first?)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Prerequisite: run 'Auth / Login as admin' first. Otherwise cookie is still the customer's and this returns 201.

item.name: Guard order: past + bad dealership -> 400 (past wins)

request.description: Both requestedStart (past) and dealershipId (unknown) are invalid. Handler checks requestedStart BEFORE dealership, so 400 REQUESTED_START_IN_PAST wins.

item.name: Guard order: bad service + bad dealership -> 404 SERVICE_TYPE_NOT_FOUND (service wins)
Booking - Conflict handling (#5/#6/#7)

exec: // Random future day, this pair uses 08:00 + 08:45 on it.

request.description: First booking of the AT-10 pair (BR-03 half-open interval). Sets a random future day at 08:00 UTC, then the follow-up 'Adjacent' request books the very next 45-minute slot (08:45) on the SAME day — which must be allowed because the intervals only touch, they don't overlap.

item.name: AT-10 Adjacent: book oil at 08:45 same day (201, half-open BR-03)

exec: pm.test('Status is 201 (adjacent slots do not overlap, BR-03)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));
pm.test('Alex assigned again (only qualified tech)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().technician.name).to.equal('Alex Chen'));

request.description: Second booking of the AT-10 pair. Starts EXACTLY when the previous one ended (08:45 = 08:00 + 45min oil-change duration). Half-open interval `[08:00, 08:45)` does NOT include 08:45, so this is allowed — expect 201.
First booking of the AT-11 pair. Books 10:00-10:45. Follow-up 'Overlap' tries 10:30-11:15 which overlaps by 15 minutes -> must fail 409.

item.name: AT-11 Overlap: book oil at 10:30 same day (409, overlaps)

exec: pm.test('code=NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN (Alex is the only qualified tech and he is busy)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().code).to.equal('NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN'));

request.description: Overlaps [10:30, 11:15) with the previous booking's [10:00, 10:45). #5's availability narrowing sees Alex is busy 10:00-10:45 and short-circuits with 409 BEFORE attempting INSERT.
First booking of the AT-08 pair. Follow-up 'Duplicate' books the EXACT SAME slot -> #5's narrowing sees Alex busy -> 409.

item.name: AT-08 Duplicate: book oil at 12:00 again (409 NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN)

exec: pm.test('NOT 500 - never surface the raw exclusion violation to the client', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.equal(500));

request.description: Exact-same-slot duplicate. Handler's #5 narrowing catches this BEFORE INSERT -> 409. If you ever saw a 500 here in the past, that was #6's EXCLUDE constraint firing without #7's retry catching it. With #5 in place, the sequential path never reaches the constraint.

item.name: #7 Race: fire two concurrent same-time bookings (never 500)

exec: // Compute a fresh future slot at 14:00 UTC.
// Fire two in parallel and collect results.
const p1 = new Promise(r => pm.sendRequest(opts, (_e, res) => r({ code: res && res.code, text: res && res.text() })));
const p2 = new Promise(r => pm.sendRequest(opts, (_e, res) => r({ code: res && res.code, text: res && res.text() })));
pm.test('Not both 201 (constraint would have failed to prevent double-book)', () => {
// The dummy top-level request (OpenAPI GET) is expected to be 200 - not part of the assertion.

request.description: Bypasses this request itself (hits OpenAPI as a no-op) - the real work is in the pre-request script, which fires TWO simultaneous /api/appointments POSTs via pm.sendRequest. This is the only way from Postman to exercise the true race that #7 catches: both requests pass #5's availability check together, both attempt INSERT, one hits the EXCLUDE constraint, and #7 retries. Expected outcome: one 201 + one 409. Never a 500. Never two 201s (would mean the constraint failed).

item.description:
Verifies availability computation (#5), DB EXCLUDE constraint (#6), and graceful retry on race (#7).

**How the tests avoid DB collisions between runs:**
Each paired test's pre-request script generates a UNIQUE random future date, so re-running the folder does not require truncating the appointments table.

**Pair sequencing matters** — run each 'Setup' request before its follow-up ('Adjacent', 'Overlap', 'Duplicate'). Postman Runner does this automatically when you run the whole folder.

**With seeded data** (1 dealership, 2 bays, 1 technician Alex Chen qualified for both service types):
- The 409 is `NO_QUALIFIED_TECHNICIAN` because Alex is the only qualified tech — he runs out before bays do.
- The `#7 race` request fires two truly concurrent bookings via `pm.sendRequest` — the second must be 409 (or 201 on the retry path), never 500.

item.name: 401 - no cookie (disable cookie jar in Settings tab)

exec: pm.test('Status is 401 (did you disable cookie jar?)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

request.description: Set Postman to skip cookies for this request: Settings tab (per-request) -> tick 'Disable cookie jar'. Then send. Expect 401 (empty body). If you get 201, cookies are still being attached from the jar.
